document_ratings.weight_rationale: Canonical race-theory chapter; 29 of 65 instances (45%) concentrated here; densest evidence for Volk-as-racial-body, enemy-as-parasite, and sacrificial obligation.
document_ratings.rationale: Sacred-object evidence is maximal: das Volk as racial organism is the chapter's central, sustained theme across blood (Blut), soil (Boden), soul (Seele), and destiny (Schicksal). Sacrificial-body evidence is present through Opfer/opfern martyrdom language (clusters 02, 06). Enemy-as-bringer-of-death is strong via PURIFICATION IS PHYSICAL DESTRUCTION and Jew-as-parasite/disease mappings. Historical-enactment alignment corroborated by subsequent Nuremberg Laws (1935) and racial-science institutionalization. Clusters 04, 05, 07, 08 have zero instances in this document; thin-evidence caveat applies.

document_ratings.weight_rationale: NSDAP founding chapter; 8 instances; establishes movement-as-sacred-cause and Führer-embodiment frame.
document_ratings.rationale: Sacred object (Volk/movement/racial mission) is strongly present. Sacrificial body evidence is moderate: Helden/Heldentum (hero/heroism) language supplies sacrificial-economy framing. Enemy construction present but less developed than in dedicated race-theory chapters. Historical alignment corroborated by NSDAP organizational history and subsequent political trajectory.

document_ratings.weight_rationale: Autobiographical formation chapter; 5 instances; important for diachronic setup but less doctrinally dense.
document_ratings.rationale: Vienna chapter supplies early Volk/racial-body formation language but does not yet develop the full sacrificial or extermination logic. Sacred-object evidence is present through Blut and Schicksal expressions; sacrificial-body and enemy-death evidence are thin. Historical alignment is primarily developmental context rather than direct enactment.

document_ratings.weight_rationale: Worldview-theory chapter; 3 instances; establishes ideological frame for racial Weltanschauung.
document_ratings.rationale: Weltanschauung chapter develops the sacred-collective frame (racial ideology as ultimate worldview) and enemy-as-contamination logic (PURIFICATION IS PHYSICAL DESTRUCTION present). Sacrificial-body evidence is limited given the small instance count. Historical alignment corroborated by ideological institutionalization in the Reich.

document_ratings.weight_rationale: State theory chapter; 7 instances; develops racial state concept and purification mandate.
document_ratings.rationale: State theory chapter establishes the racial state as sacred object and develops strong enemy-as-contamination framing through PURIFICATION IS PHYSICAL DESTRUCTION mappings. The Vernichtung cluster is well-represented. Historical alignment corroborated by subsequent racial-state legislation and bureaucratic persecution apparatus.

document_ratings.weight_rationale: Eastern Europe / Lebensraum chapter; 8 instances; explicit territorial expansion and racial elimination mandate; directly foreshadows Operation Barbarossa.
document_ratings.rationale: The Lebensraum chapter presents the most explicit territorial-elimination logic: the Volk requires eastern land for survival, and the Slavic/Jewish enemy is constructed as an existential obstacle requiring removal. Sacred-object evidence is maximal (Lebensraum as biological necessity for the racial body). Enemy-as-bringer-of-death is very strong. Historical-enactment alignment is high: Operation Barbarossa (1941) and Einsatzgruppen mass shootings directly enacted this logic. Sacrificial-body evidence is moderate via hero framing.

document_ratings.weight_rationale: Reichstag speech of January 30, 1939; 2 instances; contains the public 'prophecy' of Jewish annihilation; most historically consequential single speech in the corpus for enemy-as-bringer-of-death.
document_ratings.rationale: The Reichstag speech provides the clearest public statement of enemy-as-bringer-of-death at maximum intensity: the Jew is declared an annihilationist threat to European civilization, licensing genocide as defensive necessity. PURIFICATION IS PHYSICAL DESTRUCTION mappings are present. Historical-enactment alignment is maximal: the speech was subsequently invoked to justify mass murder at Wannsee (January 1942) and throughout the Final Solution. Sacred-object and sacrificial-body evidence is thinner given the 2-instance count; thin-evidence caveat applies to those dimensions for this document.

document_ratings.weight_rationale: USSR invasion proclamation June 22, 1941; 3 instances; state-level enactment document linking racial-body frame to military invasion and Jewish-Bolshevist enemy construction.
document_ratings.rationale: The USSR proclamation fuses Volk-survival (sacred object), Jewish-Bolshevist enemy-as-contaminant, and military mobilization into a single document of state enactment. Enemy-as-bringer-of-death is very strong. Historical-enactment alignment is maximal: the proclamation launched Operation Barbarossa, directly followed by Einsatzgruppen mass shootings. Sacrificial-body evidence is limited given the 3-instance count; the operational register foregrounds destruction over martyrdom.

hitler-01-volk-racial-body.evidence_status: strong — dominant cluster, 62% of all instances

hitler-03-jew-parasite-disease.evidence_status: strong — second-largest cluster, 25% of all instances

hitler-04-purification-surgery.evidence_status: absent — zero instances; dimension not independently scored from this cluster

hitler-06-sacrifice-martyrdom.evidence_status: thin — high salience but 3 instances, all in Vol1 Ch11

limitations: Scores are provisional and based on reviewed MIPVU annotations for 8 Hitler corpus documents.
Four of eight clusters (04, 05, 07, 08) have zero instances; those dimensions are scored from adjacent cluster evidence and document-level content rather than direct cluster representation.

Sacrificial-body score is lower than sacred-object and enemy-death scores because martyrdom language (cluster 06) is thin (3 instances); the dominant death logic is enemy-extermination, not self-sacrifice.

Historical-alignment claims cite Nuremberg Laws (1935), Wannsee Conference (January 1942), Operation Barbarossa (June 1941), and Einsatzgruppen records — these require publication-grade citations before final use.

The support rating measures Koenigsbergian framework alignment, not moral severity. The moral-equivalence guardrails in cases/x-case/validation/moral-equivalence-guardrails.json apply: structural similarity in metaphor does not establish moral equivalence with other cases.
All corpus texts are German source-language; metaphor annotation was conducted on source-language glosses per MIPVU protocol.
